reason: The dropped qualifier '基本的に' converts a hedged statement into an absolute one, but the practical guidance is identical: use the handler as-is without modification. No concrete customization scenarios are described in the source, so developers receive the same actionable direction regardless of the qualifier. The inaccuracy is real but has no meaningful effect on developer behavior in mission-critical enterprise contexts.

findings_assessment.justification: The omission of '基本的に' makes the no-change guidance absolute rather than hedged. However, the source itself provides no guidance on what exceptions might exist — it only says the handler 'can be used as-is.' Developers consulting this section receive the same actionable instruction either way. The risk of misleading a developer into missing a legitimate customization need is minimal given the handler's narrow, well-defined responsibility (clearing thread-local variables). The Phase E log shows the fix was not applied — the s3 content is identical to the pre-fix state — indicating the improvement loop silently skipped this finding without updating the file.
